channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Les philosophes doivent-ils étudier la dernière théorie cosmologique de Stephen Hawking et Thomas Hertog pour comprendre la vie et l'Univers ? Partie I
item.description: Stephen Hawking a dit une fois que, pour lui, la philosophie était morte. Pourtant, on a bien l’impression que plusieurs de ses travaux, notamment sa dernière théorie cosmologique qu’il a élaborée en collaboration avec le cosmologiste belge Thomas Hertog, ont des choses à nous dire du point de...

item.title: Judge rules DOGE used ChatGPT in a way that was both dumb and illegal
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency's cancellation of over $100 million in grants was unconstitutional, according to a ruling on Thursday. In the 143-page decision, US District Judge Colleen McMahon cites DOGE's process for eliminating grants, which involved using ChatGPT to determine if something is related to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI). The ruling, which stems […]...

item.title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra vs Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra : comment choisir le meilleur smartphone premium ?
item.description: C'est la guerre des « Ultras » ! Alors que Samsung vient tout juste de présenter sa cuvée des S26, voyons comment se défend le précédent flagship de la marque face au tout frais Xiaomi 17 Ultra.

item.title: An AI agent rewrote a Fortune 50 security policy. Here's how to govern AI agents before one does the same.
item.description: A CEO’s AI agent rewrote the company’s security policy. Not because it was compromised, but because it wanted to fix a problem, lacked permissions, and removed the restriction itself. Every identity check passed. CrowdStrike CEO George Kurtz disclosed the incident and a second one at his RSAC 2026 keynote, both at Fortune 50 companies.The credential was valid. The access was authorized. The action was catastrophic.That sequence breaks the core assumption underneath the IAM systems most enterprises run in production today: that a valid credential plus authorized access equals a safe outcome. Id...

item.title: ABC and Disney accuse Trump admin of violating First Amendment rights
item.description: ABC is accusing the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) of violating its First Amendment rights by making "major shifts in policy and practice" that the network claims will chill free speech. The network is asking the FCC to "affirm its long-standing approach to the bona fide news interview exemption" for daytime talk show The View, and […]

item.title: Anthropic wants to own your agent's memory, evals, and orchestration — and that should make enterprises nervous
item.description: Just a few weeks after announcing Claude Managed Agents, Anthropic has updated the platform with three new capabilities that collapse infrastructure layers like memory, evaluation, and multi-agent orchestration, into a single runtime.This move could threaten the standalone tools that many enterprises cobble together.The new capabilities — 'Dreaming,' 'Outcomes,' and 'Multi-Agent Orchestration' — aim to make agents inside Claude Managed Agents “more capable at handling complex tasks with minimal steering,” Anthropic said in a press release. Dreaming deals with memory, where agents “reflect” on...

item.title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
item.description: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

item.title: One of my top gaming headset picks just got a new level of personalization — Audeze's ReSkin earcup covers let you 'bring flair and personality' to your headset
item.description: Audeze has announced its ReSkin earcups, a suite of limited edition earcup designs for the Maxwell 2 headset.

item.title: InMusic will acquire Native Instruments, putting it under the same umbrella as Akai
item.description: The company also owns Moog and M-Audio, so this creates an industry juggernaut.

item.title: Un écran gaming à 540 Hz pour moins de 400 euros, c’est possible grâce à cette offre de Boulanger
item.description: Avec son Agon Pro, AOC n'y va pas avec le dos de la cuillère et propose un superbe écran pour le gaming. Ce moniteur mêle du 540 Hz et un temps de réponse de 0,03 ms pour 399,99 euros, soit une remise de 20 % par rapport à son prix d'origine.

item.title: Shoot up Xenomorphs in Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2, arriving this summer
item.description: The sequel expands the player count to four and adds new classes.

item.title: Someone out-Trumped the Trump phone
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This week, Trump Mobile has been trumped. It's been another long week without the Trump phone, but it's no longer the only nonexistent phone I have to worry about. Robot […]

item.title: Intel has reportedly signed a preliminary deal to produce chips for Apple
item.description: Is it 2006 again?

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem gets a new Leon Must Die Forever mode, out today
item.description: Capcom knows you want more.

item.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.

item.title: 'We’re going through the single largest infrastructure buildout in human history': Jensen Huang says new Nvidia AI partnership will ‘revitalize American manufacturing’
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says new partnership with Corning could be vital for nationwide advances in AI.

item.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
item.description: Apple and Intel reached a "preliminary agreement" for Intel to make chips for Apple hardware, The Wall Street Journal reported Friday. Apple had famously moved on from Intel-powered computers with its highly successful transition to Apple Silicon, but now it appears the two companies will be working closely together again. Intel appointed Lip-Bu Tan as […]...

item.title: Poland says hackers breached water treatment plants, and the US is facing the same threat
item.description: A report by Poland’s top intelligence agency accused Russia of sabotage and hacking activities against the country’s military and civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Another major Linux security flaw revealed — 'Dirty Frag' allows root on all major distros, with no patch or fix available yet
item.description: A researcher shared their findings with Linux distro maintainers, but leaked before a patch was built.

item.title: Huawei has unveiled two new kids’ wearables to add to its lineup of smartwatches — the Watch Kids X1 Series — and they could prove to be serious competition for the Garmin Bounce 2 and Fitbit Ace LTE
item.description: Huawei has announced its new Watch Kids X1 series, and they both come jam-packed with handy child safety features.

item.title: The Steam Controller’s reservation queue is open
item.description: After quickly selling out of the Steam Controller on May 4th, Valve has launched a reservation system to ensure that you can get one without too much hassle. You may not get one as quickly as you'd like to, but at least you can now easily reserve the $99 controller. If your Steam account is […]

item.title: Bluehost's new service combines ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Grok subscriptions for just $20 a month
item.description: Access all your favourite AI tools at a fraction of the usual price

item.title: The 46 best last-minute Mother’s Day gift ideas for 2026
item.description: Whether it's managing a busy home or looking out for everyone around them, moms spend a lot of time every day caring for others. This Sunday, Mother's Day, is an opportunity to return the favor, so we've rounded up practical gadgets and little luxuries that can lighten her load. This year's picks are designed to […]

item.title: US defense contractor who sold hacking tools to Russian broker ordered to pay $10M to former employers
item.description: Former cybersecurity executive Peter Williams stole several surveillance and hacking tools and sold them for $1.3 million to a Russian broker that works with Putin’s government.

item.title: Need a last-minute Mother's Day gift that she'll actually like? Amazon's Kindle Paperwhite arrives before Sunday, and it's on sale
item.description: If you still need a last-minute Mother's Day gift, Amazon has its best-selling Kindle Paperwhite on sale, and it arrives before Sunday.

item.title: Legendary Nintendo designer Takashi Tezuka is seemingly retiring from the company
item.description: Tezuka helped create the very first Mario and Zelda games.

item.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
item.description: As part of an earnings presentation on Friday, Sony shared how it's thinking about AI at the company, including many details about how it's evaluating AI as part of making PlayStation games. Generative AI has recently been showing up in bigger games - though many indie developers still reject it - and while Sony calls […]

item.title: Vocci Ring may be the first AI wearable I do not hate — it's a ring that records, transcribes, organizes, and mostly stays out of the way
item.description: This Vocci AI smart ring listens, transcribes and organizes thoughts in a way I wish my notes always did but never do

item.title: Teenage Engineering’s KO Sidekick is a mixer with fun performance effects
item.description: The EP-136 KO Sidekick is Teenage Engineering's stylish new two-channel mixer, clearly designed to connect two of its KO-II samplers for live performances and DJ sets. Though, it can connect any two audio sources, not just TE gear. The mixer leaked a couple of times, but what those leaks didn't show was that it's also […]

item.title: Apple TV's tense new thriller is a must-watch for Paradise fans as James Marsden steps into the role of a CIA agent
item.description: Disavowed is shaping up to be an exciting new addition to Apple TV's library.

item.title: Mozilla says Anthropic's Mythos Preview and other AI models helped it identify and ship 423 Firefox security bug fixes in just one month
item.description: Mozilla has patched over 400 bugs using Anthropic Mythos, but it wasn't a silver bullet.

item.title: I tested the DJI Osmo Mobile 8P — and DJI has fixed the one thing holding its smartphone gimbal back
item.description: One small improvement over its predecessor makes the Osmo Mobile 8P the new go-to small stabilizer for smartphones.

item.title: Switch 2 : Nintendo annonce une hausse de prix, une console aussi chère que la PS5
item.description: Nintendo a annoncé une hausse de prix pour la Switch 2 en Europe. Le nouveau prix entrera en vigueur à partir du 1er septembre 2026. Au Japon, les anciens modèles de Switch et les abonnements au Nintendo Online augmenteront également de prix. Cette tendance à l'augmentation des prix des consoles se renforce, après celle annoncée par PlayStation.
